heroHeadline:
Nowruz Under Fire: IDF Strikes Tehran on Persian New Year; Iran's 66th Wave Deploys Super-Heavy Missiles;
Qatar Halts All LNG Output; Brent Hits $115; Pentagon Seeks $200B; UAE Dismantles Hezbollah Network
heroSubtitle: Day 21 — Nowruz 2026 marked by IDF airstrikes on Tehran as Iranians try to celebrate the Persian New Year. Iran's 66th retaliatory wave (Operation True Promise 4) deployed five super-heavy multi-warhead missiles at Tel Aviv and US bases. UAE intercepted 7 Iranian missiles + 15 drones. Qatar was forced to halt all LNG production following Iranian counterattacks — a major global energy shock. Brent crude hit $115/bbl before retreating. Pentagon requested $200 billion in supplemental war funding. UAE dismantled a Hezbollah-Iran terrorist financial network. UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, and Japan joined statements for safe Hormuz passage. Iran's FM flatly rejected ceasefire: 'No scenario for a ceasefire.' IDF claims 70%+ of Iran's missile launchers and 85%+ of radar/air defense systems neutralized. US CENTCOM: 7,000+ targets struck since Feb 28.
footerNote: Data compiled from 100+ sources across 4 media poles as of March 20, 2026 (Nowruz). All contested claims are marked. Multi-pole sourcing active.
